channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 123 replies — 162.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 123 • Updated: 2026-06-05 19:28 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62318167
>>62318124
Nah man, there are really very few brilliant people in crypto. I worked in the industry for years, most people are average intellect or lesser than, but what's actually worse is nobody has any real work/business experience. I'm sure that'll change with time since as a result they literally all built failed/failing products, but I got the fuck out because I was so far beyond every person I met re: my work experience and I was learning absolutely nothing except how easy it is to outright scam people.

62318173
>>62318167
I admit, I had to rewrite my post I was about to fling a ton of slurs at the industry. I don't understand how people don't see it's a problem that you can go to a crypto conference, and not spend any crypto at all or interact with the blockchain in a meaningful way that makes you think, "holy cow this is the future". But I did go to a conference where one event was a scam event that stole someone's ETH.

62318186
>>62318173
In other words, I was being extremely nice in saying the industry had brilliant people. There are, just not enough to say the least.

62318188
>>62318173
At one point in my crypto "career" I wound up employed by scammers -- obviously didn't know going into it, but it became clear probably less than a year in at which point I started fast looking for my exit. They had raised 8 figs from VCs, were extracting 7 figs left and right from other crypto projects by promising them things they had no intention of building, and all these other crypto projects literally just gave us money without any contractual obligations. The founders would turn around afterwards and just say "yea, we're not going to build that", and really they never built anything. Whole operation was capital extraction, purely. But I almost couldn't blame them, because I started to realize that was 95%+ of the industry, which is why retards sitting on 9-10 figs happily gave away 7 figs to get a couple of tweets. If they could pass as "legitimate" in the eyes of retail, it drastically increased their chances of dumping enough tokens to pay for those $1-2M and then some. The whole game was either fleecing retail or fleecing the people fleecing retail. Crazy shit.

62318952
>>62318008
>The BTC network works by miners racing for calculate a hash
The Chainlink network works by nodes putting external data onchain.

>The BTC out in market came from miners selling
How is this different from Sergey selling?
Or Sergey distributing to nodes who then sell?

>miners are not going to sell BTC below operating costs
Literally any business will avoid selling at a loss.

These factors are what you claim ties the price of BTC to the usage of the network. You're a seriously stupid person.

62319004
>>62317281
>Your coin is a piece of shit
The market pumped XLM even though DTCC is explicitly pursuing "all" chains, and Stellar is just one of literally hundreds.
The market did not pump Link even though DTCC is explicitly using Chainlink in an exclusive fashion to connected those hundreds of chains.

Sounds like the piece of shit here is "the market", not the coin.

62319293
>>62312614
>>62312653
>>62312685
H1DR4 could be an idea for this task

62319302
>>62319004
LMAOOOO this nigga is either a paid shill or a schizophrenic baggie. Anyways price + chart cuckold

62319405
>>62319302
You're just proving my point: this market is a piece of shit.
It pumps XLM, but not Link.
It pumps Toncoin on a fucking name change, but not Link.
Absolute dogshit like BNB, XRP, Doge, Cardano, ... are still top fixtures.
etc.

There's a very good reason Bitcoin is down 50% while the stock market is at ATH.

62319468
wow, a thread with organic conversation. Based anons. Have a bump from yours truly

62319507
>>62319405
Tbh im happy the retards are getting flushed out of the ecosystem. Fuck the memecoin degenerates. Fuck those NFT picture mongoloids, fuck candle chasing faggots and easily mindraped casuals jumping into the most obvious scams. I got in for the tech and potential of lifting humanity to the next stage like how the invention of money created the framework of society and a bear market isn't changing shit. I don't care about price as my future and existence doesn't depend on a number on a screen even if it would be nice and could be life changing, i'm happy with life except for the wageslave part but my job isn't shitty. It would be nice to see the changes to society manifest while im alive which i'd probably care as much about as the financial gain.

62319609
>>62313866
>cll makes hundreds of millions of $ worth of money through the scale program, and this shit will continue increasing, the DTCC or swift also have private contracts through the company called SmartContract.com
wait is this true? Can I verify this somewhere?

62319647
>>62314036
this makes no sense though, if they buyback to refill the reserve it will be buy pressure on the markets contrary to before the premine has run out.

62319800
>>62319507
amen but fuck wage slaving, i literally stopped waging, i just ride bullruns, and then wait 4 years until sergey finally baby carries me to the land of "make it" even though i consider myself blessed alreafy because i didnt wage for the last 2 years thx to sergey.

fuck all the degen retards who ape into shitcoins i hope they all burn and fuck off, meanwhile us linkies are guaranteed to stay until retirement because chainlink has nowhere to go but become huge and central to all of the future of finance ammennn

>>62319609
go to linkedin and look whos the ceo of smartcontract.com there are 9 employees

>>62319647
why not? thet are right now using the preminted tokens to pay for expenses and subsidizing all while having a reserve, bas8cally the exceess money is put into a dca into the reserve so they can continue this game forever, also nobody said theyd only buy 1m every week forever, i hope they increase it

62319862
>>62312685
I don’t think there’s much OTC Link to be bought. In any case even OTC buys support price over time. And you cat seriously believe that big investors are accumulating significant amounts of Link over the years while price keeps going down, that’s not possible.

62319875
>>62312881
I think it’s still a pretty important token in the crypto space, but that doesn’t necessarily make the token valuable. That’s why it causes so much heartache here.

62319882
>>62319800
>go to linkedin and look whos the ceo of smartcontract.com there are 9 employees
obviously I'm not asking to verify the fact that there are 9 employees you absolute retard

62319898
>>62313883
>he forgot about postmine

62319917
>>62319862
>you cat seriously believe that big investors are accumulating significant amounts of Link over the years while price keeps going down, that’s not possible.
It's extremely possible. Link has millions in volume every day, people could buy ungodly amounts in a matter of weeks. And as the price tries to go up, Bitcoin simply dumps and dumps and dumps again to keep it low. Like when it kept Link under $9 for over a year.

62319918
>>62319882
i ncan't fucking post links on 4chan you fucking mongoloid
but the company does exist and it's the one having private deals with swift
>rocketreach*dot*co*slash*smartcontract-profile_b54a3c45f6db61a8

62319921
>>62319875
Meanwhile over half the coins with higher market caps are literally useless yet more valuable.

62322856
>>62318952
>>>The BTC out in market came from miners selling
>How is this different from Sergey selling?
>Or Sergey distributing to nodes who then sell?

https://etherscan.io/address/0x9b1c1752b6b6eaf62a8c491a8681b32c1f7ecde9#tokentxns

Can you prove that all the link tokens Chainlink Labs moved from their noncirculating token wallets to Binance went straight to the node operators? If you can't prove it, your analogy falls apart.

62323005
>>62316188
>Why did XLM pump +80% on lesser DTCC news then?

i don't know dude, maybe bc their liquidity is pretty thin

how many times do i have to fucking tell you people that the market isn't fair

you are essentially complaining that your scam isn't working as good as it used to. you realize that right?

>>62316251
>Yes anon, Bitcoin dumping every time Link has big news is my fault.

you are proving my point
if you're convinced there's a conspiracy to stop your gains, why would you continue holding
you are basically saying you know for sure it's impossible to make money, and yet you continue the same path

einstein would refer to that as insanity

62323033
>>62316263
>XLM is still higher than before the pump started, LINK prints new monthly lows
>if what the shill claimed was true, LINK would exhibit similar performance


the kid you are arguing against actually thinks he can debate his way into changing reality

i don't know if it's a combination of autism, naivety, or he's just plain retarded, but that's his thought process. he literally believes if he can show people how the market is "wronging" him, it will change things

62323065
>>62316401
>I bought in prior to mainnet so I am well into the green

fact check: you're down 80% and desperate

>and am living off my staking rewards.

LOL

fact check: you have enough money to survive 5-10 years and then will move back home if you have even left yet

>I sympathize with you anon since if this were XRP,

fact check: it's not xrp

> You're doomed to wait till after the Clarity Act

fact check: clarity act will have zero upward pressure on link price

>when banks finally get their markets up and running

what the fuck does that even mean. banks run fine every single day

>Real World Assets NFT treading

HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHA

62323184
>>62316444
>Chainlink network must use the LINK token to secure the network

fact check: chainlink network currently operates fine without link token. (offshore labs company makes offchain deals and full staking version with slashing not released nor coming any time soon)

>>62316605
>. This program is a way to grow these companies, the actual users of the network and get them up and running and profitable which would drive more usage of the Chainlink network which should drive the value up.

fact check: these companies are not growing. they are grifts with no users, no profit, and have no value accrual to the link token

>>62316776
>duuuude chicken and eggs

that's hilarious zach still posts here. you belong in jail

62323264
>>62317156
>oh cool adem's here. serious question why do you live on this board 24/7?

don't project you little fat skinny weirdo

62323308
>>62317182
>you're very smart, because while i read you i have thoughts that come up, and you answer those thoughts. bene lurking 4chan since 2007 or some shit i've learnt over the years to spot smart / legit people, and you are one, it's rare to meet good people


fact check: wow this is getting pretty gay

>>62317182
>they keep fudding for absolutely no reason for all these years

what exactly do you mean by the term "fud"
are you talking about saying what the literal price is, or what's actually going on? they call that reality

>>62317656
>duuuude the world went to shit after covid
>To bring in this new system that Link facilitates. And we get to be on top while the trash get fucked. It’ll be a lot more meaningful cuz we will be able to dab.

HAHAHAHAAHAHAA

62323333
So many deranged cuckolds are going to HODL this shitcoin until its back to $2

62323719
when does the humiliation end, stinkies
when do you finally rise up and say, NO

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 71 replies — 154.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 71 • Updated: 2026-06-05 19:46 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62315194
IT IS ALREADY BACK TO 64K AND NORMALIZE ABOVE THE BOTTOM.

THR BUYROG REPEATS ITSELF: THE BOTTOM IS 65K.

If you bought it, hold.
If you haven't, it is time.
If you sold, leave.

62315253
>>62311487
I am rugged.

62315328
>>62315253
It is only a bulltrap if you sell the bottom.

If you got rugged, please never return to BTC. You are not financially literate, and people like you give BTC a bad rep.

I wish this entire board would fucking burn, every single asset class is twofaced and waiting for BTC to fail.

62315335
How rekt in anus is OP right about now?

62315341
>>62309503
I am gonna put my money where my mouth is as buy 15k in BTC at current price and post proof here.

BUYROG is never wrong

62315377
>>62309503
nah, I can't feel the doom, people are expecting sixty-something. It's going to mid 50s

62318708
>>62315194
Are you sure?

62318770
Try $35,000
I'm buying at $38,500

62318780
What about the quantum fud tho?

62318798
>>62309503
I can see it hitting 3 digits again, maybe even 2 digits. Obvious sell signal. Bottomless shitcoin.

62318815
>>62312131
More like 20k

62319815
>>62318798
>I can see it hitting 3 digits again, maybe even 2 digits.
LULLLLLLLL

62319876
>>62309503
I'm afraid it will be lower, we're only halfway in the bear market

62320358
>>62309503
>65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

62320385
So far, there is no reason not to believe in the 4 year cycle. And the 4 year cycle tells US you should be buying below the 200 week MA. We just hit It. You should start accumulating right now

62320391
I LOVE those heckin cope threads. Like if some retard making a strong statement on this board is actually going to influence price action. Your brains have been fried by a decade of shitcoining and pump n dumping on other anons. Deal with the real world you absolute goy trash meats. We're going to 2 digits for btc wether u believe it or not.

62320405
>>62309503
BTC is not Bitcoin and basically worthless. Sheep will figure it out eventually, until then they follow the pedo coin BTC congrats

62320466
>>62320405
>BTC is not Bitcoin and basically worthless!!111111111
>my XYZ coin (0.0003 BTC) is real bitcoin!!!!!111111

62320468
>>62309503
The city of OP

62320500
>>62320466
>inb4 BCH is not Bitcoin either, only BSV is

62320588
>>62320500
only Bit-Diamond XD
at least they changed hash afair to not compete for same ASICs (with predictable result)

62320857
>>62320358
Good. At least now the dipshits will sell the bottom.

If you think you are getting the upperhand, you are not.

It is better for BTC to get rid of the financially illiterate than it to be worth 200k but held by imbeciles trying to scam each other.

Bitcoin is a storage of value. Want proof? Zoom out the chart.

We are winning the fight against bullshit Fiat currency, dwarven suckers, and boomer indexes.

Everyone talking shit here WISHES they had bought in years ago. Sour grapes everywhere

The BUYROG remains.

>>62320391
>cope thread

I made free money to anyone who wasn't dumb enough to now sell the bottom and make this a sucker's rally.

>>62320405
Was waiting for the inevitable BTC will go to zero comment.

It is the bottom signal.

62320897
>>62309535
>I don't need proof
>BTC 5k below your prediction.

Faggot.

62320920
>>62309503
OHNONONONONONONONONONONONONO LMAOOO

62323245
Any last words, OP?

62323584
>>62323245
>Any last words, OP?

62323600
>>62323584
>OP rn

62323880
>>62323245
>>62323584
>>62323600
YOU LOST, TRANNY

It doesn't matter if Bitcoin falls to 59k, I repeat what I said: it is good riddance that the yuppies go find another con to shove down people's throats because they are overleveraged and undereducated. No one wants their store value reserve being speculated on by retards who laugh as they sell the bottom, then complain they can't make money.

Stupidity is a tax on success.

Also, keep in mind, BTC might be 6% loss in a day, SILVER and GOLD are doing WORSE. You can't compare BTC performance against stock because it is not apples to apples, BTC can only be compared with other crypto in its asset class, and other storage value investment like PMs. And guess what? It still blows all of them out of the water, and the trade volume of BTC evaluated in any desk is far greater than all of crypto and rock collections combined. BTC brockerage platforms exist by the thousands, how many dealers of PMs exist?

I will put this even simpler for bear trolls: read the Bible, flip it open to the parable of the talents, and read what is said about the one faggot who thought he was smug and clever because he neither won, nor lost the money loaned to him. This is you, this is the whole bear market.

I am screencapping this whole shit, I have blood in my eyes. I'm going laughing my way to the bank enjoying this fire sale of BTC owned by the eternal underclass of statist peasants.

BTC will be 70k by the end of the month. Hell, it will be 60k+ by the end of the day.

If I called the bottom 10% above, it is because I overestimated the intelligence of the morons that do me a favor by forgetting this asset class exists.

May they buy ETFs and go hold the bag of billionaire scammers.

I repeat:
>if you bought, hold
>if you haven't, enjoy the sale
>if you sold, we are better without you

62323927
>>62323880
>I am screencapping this whole shit, I have blood in my eyes

62323956
>>62323880

item.title: BUY NOW — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BUY NOW

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-05 20:10 UTC

OP

THAT WAS THE BOTTOM

Early replies (first 3)

62324009
>>62323982

62324016
>>62323982
that was it, bears will be on suicide watch

62324070
>>62324016
>your shitcoins down 20% in one week
>bears on suicide watch after a 1.5% dead cat bounce

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62324090
>>62323982
That was indeed the bottom nigga. Thug life didn't sell shit.

Live the life of a thug nigga until the day I die
Live the life of a boss player (All eyes on me) 'cause even gettin' high
All eyes on me
Live the life of a thug nigga until the day I die
Live the life of a boss player 'cause even gettin' high

62324111
>>62323982
Hell yeah brother

62324120
>>62323982
YOU'RE RIGHTTTTTTT IT WAS!!!!!!! FUUUUUUUCCCCCCCKKKKKKKKKKKKKKKK!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

62324130
>>62323982
WHERE THE FUCK IS EVERYONE?!
We go below 60k and come back up above it not once but TWICE and all the big money is just sucking their own dicks and not doing jack shit!!!
Man this market is such a fucking weak sister sometimes...

62324206
>>62323982
Oh... you were serious

62324260
>>62324206
feeling nervous? its a fast ride to 80k

62324262
>>62324206
>61k
YES WE ACTUALLY FUCKING WERE

item.title: USDT flips ETH — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
USDT flips ETH

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-05 19:59 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62323404
>>62323391
at this rate its gonna flip BTC

62323434
Crypto is healing. The only legit use case for crypto are stablecoins.
I hope we can have a top 10 with only stablecoins in the coming years.

62323466
>>62323404
until someone remembers, 15%+ of this trash's 'reserve' is worthless BTC.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62323883
>>62323434
>The only legit use case for crypto are stablecoins.
I feel like that should be more obvious. Its cryptocurrency, its meant to be used to pay for things, not a speculative asset.

62323928
>>62323391
ze actual flippening kek

62323962
3 digit shitcoin eoy

62324025
Tether bros! Are we breaking out of the multi-year $1 resistance?

62324113
>>62323883
Its quite interesting. cointards dont seem to get it but the *point* of ethereum requires that the value of the coin be low. ethereum working as intended means that coins are exchanging hands at the monetary rate required to pay for the compute to run the network + a small %. I do not understand why anybody would expect the price of ethereum to go up. it is antithetical to the purpose of the machine.

This is all a good thing by the way. This is the pathway to actual intrinsic coin value. The value of the ethereum coin being tied to actual meaningful data processing work, that is.

item.title: ZEC exploit sell — 52 replies — 28.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
ZEC exploit sell

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-06-05 18:25 UTC

OP

Active ongoing Zcash exploit right now, dumping fast

Early replies (first 3)

62317133
>>62317117
You know what is funny, this whole "exploit" is fake and gay, there was only positive bews in media about zec for months.... And its the same fake shit, now they will just reverse it and it will be 100% negative news for the jews pndd it, so they can shirt it down and when they turned off the fud button in the media they can reaccumukate it and zec will be the bestest thing gigahyoerbrutal muh ultraprivacy shitcoin again..... Jews are ez!

62317139
>>62317133
In short: exit liquidity hunting the goyim.

62317152
>>62317139
+1 they are pribably the same cartel behind the hype and world coin pnds too.... As i said jews are ez, dont play their game and stay safe out in the wilderness my fellow magalanians!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62318088
>>62317117
is this bullish for my octra though

62318099
>>62317133
>this whole "exploit" is fake and gay
no it isn't, the retards designing the zero knowledge system didn't realize there was a gaping hole in their logic. these vulnerabilities sit undetected because no one bothers to verify anything these days

62318126
>>62318099
by the way, there is NO way to prove that this exploit was not used by anyone, because it is both undetectable and unlimited. that breaks every trust assumption in a zero knowledge system. in a rational world, this coin would go to zero and stay there.

62318153
>>62317117
holy fuck, why didn't I care when you posted, I could've made some money. Well I guess I'm making less money. Free short to 200 at least

62318157
>>62317975
anywhere between 200 and 60 dollars

62318176
>>62318126
$1000 EOY easy

62318272
reminder to everyone to zoom out to "all" on zcash and see how the devs started this whole shitshow with one enormous dump on the market. zcash still has the record for the largest crash of all time and it was immediately upon launch as the devs stuffed their pockets and fled. but since then there's a whole new (third) world of suckers

62318289
A new generation of privacy baggies was born. Just wait until people figure out that all of Monero's previous forks can be cracked by quantum in the future.

62318290
>>62318272
check the market cap graph

62318339
>>62318289
Only the active fork matters with XMR, by the time you deanonymize forks from years ago there is no useful adjacent information.

62318374
>>62317207
They can run a tally on flows into and out of the pool and will probably be installing a turnstile in the coming weeks. This will almost certainly either a) prove that the exploit occurred because the exploiter will move their counterfeit coins out of the pool before the turnstile can be installed, and we'll see that out > in, or b) nothing will happen, and once the turnstile is installed the worst that could happen is that the possible exploiter, who has been in a coma the past month and unable to react to the disclosure of the vulnerability, wakes up and moves some of their coins out, hitting the turnstile limit and proving that the exploit had occurred. Late migrators from the pool get scheisted and then we return to business as usual.

There is also c) which is that with Opus 4.8, mythos, etc it's conceivable that there are infinite vulnerabilities to be exploited and nobody will ever trust shielded pools and zk tech again. Same thing as is happening in slow motion with defi protocols right now. Such that even if there was never an exploit, it doesn't matter, the fact that some rando with $1000 worth of tokens and the latest version of Opus can potentially find exploits like this means game over for basically all crypto and anything else that relies on trustless execution.

62318429
oh god the zec defenders are here.... I really hope you're paid shills rather than desperate baggies, I couldn't witness that much heartbreak

62318550
>>62318429
Top Kek, fuck this kike run shitcoin that literally has a tranny behind the wheel

62318983
>>62317297
it was indeed annoying, especially if you sold early like i did. BUT deep down i knew something was off. It really felt like a complete artificial pump. It went too fast from almost dead to this is the new bitcoin. i don't like leverage but i really give some thought about shorting it, although it was a gamble seeing how it moved up randomly. Didn't go it and glad i didn't might have been scamwicked anyway even if i was right.

62318986
>>62318157
Kek my nigga, it's going back to 20$, screenshot this. That was its price 1.5 year ago by the way.

62319007
>>62318374
Anyway this goes, what a fucking disaster. Sounds like the shows over boys, the music has stopped and there are few chairs left

62319113
>>62318983
faggot

62319185
You faggots have the memory of a goldfish. Take a look at the chart, ZEC's entire history is nothing but pump and dump cycles. Nobody actually uses ZEC for its intended purpose (XMR monopolizes that use case). It has a CEO that openly schmoozes with 3-letter agencies and a hard-left trannie as lead dev, it was never going to be anything more than a glorified memecoin for whales to play with.

Keep getting dumped on, its very entertaining.

62319489
>>62319185
>ZEC's entire history is nothing but pump and dump cycles
This is true, and I would even go as far as saying the cause for the dumps are planned too.

This “exploit” was the planned rationale for dumping before the pump from $30 even began. You need an apparent reason that everyone is selling their coins, but ZEC can’t be “dead” because they’ll want to pump it again in a few months/years.

So the exploit is discovered, VCs dump their coins and realize gains. ZEC goes to like $50 again. Then the new shielded pool is created, “oh we fixed everything”, and another pump is orchestrated.

Then another hack.
Over and over again.

Perfect “M”-shaped chart patterns for VCs to extract from.
>M for Monero

62319659
>>62317117
AI killing crypto daily

62319913
>I discovered this bug two days after it was live back in 2022 and have been exploiting it ever since... I am a multi-trillionaire and nobody noticed that I massively inflated the amount of ZEC in circulation by selling obscene amounts at $35 ... Then it pumped to $700 and still nobody noticed!
This whole thing is a nothingburger temporary setback for ZEC. The bug wasn't exploited by anyone, you would have witnessed massive daily sell pressure not the pump that defied the rest of the market that we saw.

62319916
>>62319659
what coin claude will kill next?

62319930
Okay, anons, when do I close my shorts??? it can't be this easy, right? O_O. I'm making so much money is unreal

62319941
>>62319930
same lmfao

62319962
>>62319659
probably same half-truth bullshit as the "mythos" story to fuel the ai mania
(fuck ztrash though)

62320303
>>62318374
>trust shielded pools and zk tech again.
Throwing the baby (zk) out with the bathwater (shielded pools). And it's the combination of the two that's particular to this disaster

62320400
>>62319913
>the pump that defied the rest of the market that we saw.
Are we still pretending that ZEC pumps are totally organic?

Also, why are so many ZEC shills from Nigeria? Can somebody plz explain?

62321501
>>62317117
Is this why Zcash was being pimped so hard on this board for so many months?

62322851
>>62320400
>Also, why are so many ZEC shills from Nigeria? Can somebody plz explain?

Imagine being exit liquidity for a bunch of ebola niggers KEK

Biz always surprises me

62322907
>>62318374
>They can run a tally on flows into and out of the pool
All this means is that if someone minted shielded zcash and withdraws it all, the last X people in the shielded pool will be unable to unshield until they convince someone else to shield and have their shielded tokens locked indefinitely until they get someone else to add to the pool so they can unshield. It breaks the entire shield system and since that's the only thing ZEC claims to have that makes it the regulatory darling over XMR, having to remain in the shielded pool forever kills the entire token by nuking its compliance and leaving all users operating in the pool with potentially infinite fake ZEC

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-05 19:54 UTC

i sold at 115K

62321552

62321561
>>62321552

fuck these baggies

>>62321535

62321913

62322381

62324062

item.title: IT'S LITERALLY OVER FOR CRYPTO — 9 replies — 19.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT'S LITERALLY OVER FOR CRYPTO

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-05 19:46 UTC

OP

>t.Trading since 2017

Early replies (first 3)

62323541
>>62323490
Weak shit!

62323595
If you have been trading since 2017, you should know that BTC always dumps at least 80% from top to bottom.
$126k in October 2025, $25k in October 2026. Simple as.

62323622
>>62323595
dumps and pumps getting smaller actually.. this time the bottom is 47k .. after that crypto has one anemic bullrun and then dies forever

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62323659
>>62323595
wtf anon called the top on the exact day almost two years in advance

62323737
>>62323622
Nah, ~$25k bottom in 2026, ~$140k top in 2029. Law of diminishing returns.

>>62323659
And picrel is him now, daily posting about the $26k bottom since last year

62323853
>>62323490
I voted for this

62323861
gtfo tatefag

62323912
>>62323595
I WON'T MISS THIS ONE. FUCK YOU DOOMERS, THIS IS MY GENERATIONAL WEALTH!!!! AAAAH CRASH TO SUB 20K ALREADY AAAAH!

62323959
>>62323622
Crypto as a whole is fucked. BTC and ETH hit ATHs while everything else had already dumped only to never fully recover again. Even if ETH had another run up, most of the shit out there has been nuked. There are no more betas nor is there any more liquidity coming in. No one cares about this shit anymore. I really think it’s over

62318691

62322929

62323044

item.title: ETH is 5% away from losing the #2 spot to USDT — 18 replies — 17.7/hr

ETH is 5% away from losing the #2 spot to USDT

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-05 19:12 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62320467
reminder USDT barely backs up 1% of their reserves

62320470
>>62320467
It's a huge loss for ETH regardless

62320480
>>62320448
thats insane

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62320663
>>62320480
it's insane how much it got slaughtered since its ATH. it's getting sold off for almost a year now.

62320681
>>62320448
so is getting cheap?

62320994
Remember the flippening narrative? Instead we get the floppening.

62321017
>>62320994
Kek, and from a erc20 token aswell.

62321039
>>62320448
that nigger usdt better get out of eth's way

62321428
Only 1% away now

62321508
>>62320467
It's a sable coin it dosen't need any reserves as long as people keep swaping crypto into stables instead of fiat they are fine

62321529
usdt doesnt even pump who cares about stables

62321543
ETH losing the #2 is almost as bad as BTC losing the #1 spot.

It sets a new precedent and people will view it very differently from that point on.

62321607
>>62321543

62321728
It went under for about a minute but bounced back.

62321771
eh yo, where my efferiumz homeys at?

62323188
>>62321728

62323322
>>62320448

62323517

item.title: No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this. — 143 replies — 51.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this.

Open thread • Replies: 143 • Updated: 2026-06-05 17:25 UTC

OP

Saylor sells 32 BTC out of 843,000+ BTC and anon starts acting like Bitcoin is dead.

You're not a real BTC holder.

Early replies (first 3)

62299202
why did he sell 32 btc tho?

62299205
>>62299202
to get a higher rating from some corporate wagie on wall street. he was showing his btc holdings are unencumbered and can be sold at will.

62299325
Didn't he tell people to never sell?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62316059
>>62313253
I guess it depends on what you're looking for, but Monero has built a pretty strong reputation over the years.

62316066
>>62314048
Pretty much. The network treats everyone's coins the same.

62316193
Holy shit people are so low IQ.
>why did he sold?
>why does he keep buying btc
>how will he pay dividends?
>he is about to get liquidated
The cope will be unreal when coffezillafags realize they punched above their weight class here.

62317904
>>62305458
What? Lol

62317954
>>62313977
Just get all the corn from OTC retards selling then pump it to 1MM by buying on exchanges.

62318035
>>62313462
Aave TVL is down

62318553
>>62318035
TVL naturally goes up and down with the market. I think product development and actual usage matter more over the long run.

62318556
>>62317954
Just do it yourself

62318626
>>62316045
you think btcfi will win this cycle? Narratives come and go

62318638
>>62317954
STOP SMOKING weed

62319016
>>62318626
Maybe, but I think there's a difference between a narrative and actual utility. If people find more ways to use their BTC, that could last longer than the hype.

62319024
>>62316193
The funny thing is Bitcoin has survived way bigger FUD cycles than this.

62319028
>>62314204
When? Lol

62319580
>>62299202
Tax loss harvesting and >>62301590
S&P inclusion by the end of the year.

62319684
>>62299205
>he was showing his btc holdings are unencumbered and can be sold at will.
He showed that by selling just 0.004% of his holdings, the value tanked by >10%. That'll for sure inspire confidence from the rating agencies, right?

62319732
>>62312873
Do you think it's reliable enough?

62319738
>>62319684
Explain it well kek

62319775
>>62319684
They sold bitcoin in 2022 as well. It's not new.

62319840
>>62319775
but they rebought, you retard, that was for tax losses and not to pau dividends. Btw he only has enough cash to pay for more 6 months of dividends, so he needs either to dilute his shares or dump btc, either one is going to cause a fucking death spiral.

62320328
>>62319840
>so he needs either to dilute his shares or dump btc, either one is going to cause a fucking death spiral.
Oh no. Kek. Lmao even.
They're going to...checks notes...do the model they've said they're going to do. How awful.
The retardation never ceases to amaze me. We're in for a wild ride.

62320461
>>62301590
ok but why 32 and not 3.2?

62320567
crypto is done
it's more worthwhile to put your compute resources into ai instead

62321188
>>62319732
I don't think names like GoMining, Ledger, a16z crypto, and others would get involved for no reason. That's why I think it's pretty solid.

62321234
>>62320567
AI is definitely hot right now, but people have called crypto dead more times than I can count.

62321243
>>62318638
That's funny

62321361
>>62320461
I assume there was some accounting or operational reason behind it rather than just picking a random number.

62321519
>>62320567
Compute resources have nothing to do with the value of the asset

62321562
>>62299205
Well it seems he failed

62321836
>>62301632
>Microsoft, Coca-Cola or Boeing don’t sell their intellectual property; it doesn’t mean this IP has no value, that it’s ignored by rating agencies, or that it’s not real.
The difference is those companies make money out of their IP and that proves its value. The only thing that "proves" BTC has value is its sale price and if no one is trying to move significant quantities then that's when the investors start questioning if the market cap is even real, because it's all fun and games when Joe Blow is selling fractional amounts for the day trading value, but less fun when an institution moves 1000 and the price collapses 25%.

62322377
>>62314048
But it affects its image when he effectively becomes the face of it.

item.title: IT WAS SUPPOSED TO BE A STORE OF VALUE — 37 replies — 31.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT WAS SUPPOSED TO BE A STORE OF VALUE

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-06-05 19:02 UTC

OP

IT WAS SUPPOSED TO BE A STORE OF VALUE

Early replies (first 3)

62317265
>>62317251
It is a store of value, it's just that the store is out of stock.

62317267
>>62317251
You were supposed to be a woman.

62317316
>>62317251
He was just a fuckin kid

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62317419
>>62317251
Well it’s losing value. Fast.

62317425
>>62317419
buyback cheaper next year !
>problem solved

62317429
1 BTC = 1 BTC, retards

62317441
Might as well walk in here naked staring back like that you know I like that shit.

62317450
>>62317251
BTC is a store of value in the same way a diaper is a store of value.

62317492
>>62317350
>PISS

62317495
>>62317251
Wanting something to be true doesn't make it true.

62317513
>>62317251
>store of value
>notoriously volatile

62317543
>>62317441
These threads got me jerking off while I'm looking away from my btc short. Peak comfy rn, I bust ropes to the baggies copes.

62319615
Its a trillion dollar asset. Even if Saylor had to sell every bitcoin he only holds $65 Billion atm which is basically nothing.

62319667
>>62317429
This, so much this

62319683
>>62317265
My life savings are melting away and you're making jokes? Fuck you.

62319685
>>62317251
It is. You have to average it on 4 years though.

62319818
>>62317251

dishonest fuck

you wanted to price to rise further so you could dump it on the next sucker

but it turns out you ended up being the last sucker in line and you are currently getting omega dumped on.

rat poison squared.

62319822
>>62319818
the price*

62319839
>>62317251
They don't call it goygold for nothing

62319927
>>62317251
but muh ledger!

62320008
>>62317251
Sucks to suck

62320062
>>62317429
1 cow dung = 1 cow dung

62321514
jews won

62321539
>>62319615
What do you think the price elasticity is?

62321549
>>62319818
That's what "store of value" means. It should rise at least with the rate of price inflation

62321574
>>62317251
Bloody Friday Anon

62321789
>>62317320

62321818
If we count inflation, then BTC is basically back at the $30k value of 2020

62322681
>>62321549
his intent is not for it to rise at least with the rate of price inflation it's to dump it on the next idiot.

62322698
retards actually thought they'd get rich by "investing" in digital nothing? holy fuck hahahhahahahahhaha

62322708
It was supposed to be digital p2p cash...

62322910
>>62322681
Those two things are not mutually exclusive; in fact the former is kinda meaningless without the latter.

62323377
>>62319683

item.title: What happens to SPCX once the Chinese start successfully landing rockets? — 39 replies — 27.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
What happens to SPCX once the Chinese start successfully landing rockets?

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-06-05 18:48 UTC

OP

The Chinese have over a dozen Falcon 9 clones and 4 Starship clones in development

Early replies (first 3)

62322114
>>62322047
Isn’t this why it’s going public now? They’re going to dump on us.

62322119
>>62322047
same as is happening to his other piece of trash.
they'll always have Twitter tho. Not even the chinks would want to replicate that.

62322125
they don't have the raptor engine though, they can build their rockets but will take years to match the cutting edge monster that is the raptor series of engines

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62322259
>>62322235
aren't they hyping SPCX on AI rather than Rockets n sheeeit tho? Not that the long-term outlook for xAI looks any better.

62322277
>>62322119
>no Twitter
So you don't know about QQ, Weibo, and all of their Twitter clones which continually stuck because of censorship?

62322295
>>62322235
china can't even replicate jet engines from rolls royce, its not as easy as you think, they then need to stack 30 of them together to match starship, that's a lot of complex plumbing that could go wrong in so many ways

62322328
>>62322259
Everyone I've talked to is by far most impressed by the self-landing rockets. They attribute it to unique genius that makes Einstein seem mediocre in comparison. Bezos' rocket exploding reinforced this view.

62322361
>>62322328
bezos just got his first lesson in trying to compete with spacex

62322367
>>62322151
no

62322411
>>62322295
I'm by no means an expert on this matter, but I think your information on Chinese tech might be about 25 years out of date. The Chinese long ago moved on from the Rolls Royce Spey to better, independently developed engines.

Replicating Starship is obviously a big engineering challenge, but my point is that, when the Chinese start landing rockets, investors are going to realize that the Chinese will be able to follow SpaceX's path; it's just a matter of time.

62322450
>>62322188
Does Starlink really have much of a moat? China's Qianfan is probably going to attain initial operating capability in 2027. It won't be able to serve as many customers as Starlink, but it will be an alternative, and it's only going to keep growing. If China replicates Falcon 9 several times over, investors will realize that China's plan to launch 28,000 telecommunications satellites is achievable, and that eventually the space telecommunications market might become saturated several times over.

62322455
>>62322411
NO!!!!! The Chinese don't care and SpaceX really doesn't care. They will land the rockets and nothing will happen. NOTHING! You think everyone using the SpaceX rockets will just jump ship and partner up with China? Are you fucking retarded? Think about all the military applications that go through SpaceX. Think about all the payloads.

This retarded notion that if China makes a successful copy, everyone will jump ship. NO!! China is an adversarial country. No smart company is going to grant China a multi-billion payload contract when SpaceX has a proven track record.

62322459
>>62322411
and then what, take over the american market like byd has done with tesla?

62322472
>>62322177
Yeah, but stocks are supposed to be forward-looking, so when investors realize it's going to happen, it should be immediately reflected in the stock price

62322474
>>62322459
>take over the american market like byd has done with tesla?
see >>62322367
Its fake sales as per usual. How are you still dumb?

62322485
>>62322459
Qianfan will surely be banned in the American market, like BYD, but it will compete with Starlink in much of the rest of the world.

62322500
>>62322455
Government payloads are only a small fraction of SpaceX's revenue. Starlink is the cash cow, and the US can't ban Chinese Starlink competitors outside the US.

62322512
>>62322459
100% tariffs is just shouting at the tide to retreat. At some point, even Burger consoomers begin noticing everyone else in the fucking world is paying 50% less for (arguably) better cars. And they start demanding to know why.

62322515
>>62322500
>Starlink is the cash cow
starlink is the military conttract

62322545
>>62322485
if they can do it at scale and cheaply sell it, good for them, but i don't think china will be able to subsidize this for any length of time since europe is also calling them out for what they are doing with evs and europe is a major customer compared to the rest of the third world, china will have to choose btn self sufficiency of everything and dominating a few select industries

62322546
>>62322515
Starshield is the military-oriented product

62322554
>>62322500
>Starlink is the cash cow
while I consider Starlink perhaps one of Musk less obviously complete junk enterprises, its still just an argument against gravity.
and gravity (invariably) wins.

62322558
>>62322047
The US and the rest of the world will welcome Chinese space companies obviously. Obviously the Ubited States will tell musk to fuck off and build a Chinese headquarters in the United States and give China billions to run our space programs. Fuck you think will happen OP?

62322590
>>62322545
The car industry is an important employer in Europe, whereas the space communications industry is not. I don't see why Europe wouldn't want maximum competition in space communications in order to drive the price as far down as possible.

The Chinese don't need to subsidize anything to offer service to Europe or any other region. Once the satellites are in orbit, the marginal cost of extending coverage to a new region is essentially zero.

>china will have to choose btn self sufficiency of everything and dominating a few select industries
This isn't really related to the thread topic, but it is an interesting thing to discuss. China has more people than North America, Europe and Japan combined, so I don't see why they need to specialize on a few select industries.

62322603
>>62322558
I think Chinese Starlink competitors will be banned in the US market but not in other markets

62322644
>>62322590
there are communication companies in europe, no, china will need to compete with them too, china produces fewers ev vehicle than toyota exported cars, to keep this running, the govt is running massive subsidies because demand isn't that high, especially for their high end evs, europe and america have imposed tarrifs on these cars and they are not likely to relent, same thing will happen with telecommunications, isn't nokia a telecommunication company nowadays?

62322676
another day, another lie
I'm not certain they won't postpone SPCX takeoff. Conditions do not look promising and the S&P telling them to fuck off and abide the fucking rules has to be a blow.

62322696
>>62322644
Nokia and Ericsson are telecommunications equipment providers. They are not service providers.

Space communications are supposed to provide coverage where terrestrial networks are underdeveloped or unable to fill demand, such as in remote regions or on aircraft, not compete directly with terrestrial communications. Terrestrial communications are much more capable, wherever proper terrestrial infrastructure investments have been made.

62322747
>>62322696
on aircrafts, yes starlink is now on aircrafts, you think airbus will allow china on their aircraft after what they are trying to do with their aircraft manufacturing companies trying to replace them, i think europe has pretty much made its stance with china, if china wants to dominate these technologies, it will have to sell to india, south america and africa, but make products that these countries can buy cheaply, and i don't think it will be able to scale

62322792
>>62322747
As mentioned, space communications is not a major employer in Europe.

I think Airbus will offer whatever the customer wants, and I think operators will want as many communications providers as possible in order to increase competition and drive down costs. If an antenna for a communications provider the operator wants does not come preinstalled on an Airbus aircraft, the operator can just retrofit the aircraft.

62322836
>>62322119
Tesla's stock price has been remarkably resilient in the face of increasing competition, and that's why it's by no means obvious what will happen to SpaceX's stock price

62322940
>>62322792
europe's networks will also be subsidised to compete with china, europe is also pretty well connected compared to the third world, and internet is pretty cheap nowadays, this is a hard sell

62323199
>>62322047
The US space program is a weapon. There will be no shortage of funding. Maybe they will be forced to merge with each other if things get real bad but they wont go away.
